title: Repayment Transaction
description:
Is an Action that records or executes the payment of principal, interest, or both toward an outstanding obligation. It represents any event that reduces the borrowers debt or fulfills repayment terms.
Comment:
Money Transfer Transaction realizing the repayment of a loan

is the additional information to the money transfer transaction, referring to the information contained in the instruction to repay, given by the customer.

RepaymentTransactionIdentification.description: A Repayment Transaction Identification is the unique reference code assigned to a repayment execution event. It distinguishes one repayment transaction (the actual debit/credit movement) from other repayment instructions or arrangements,

RepaymentTransactionPayerReference.description: A Repayment Transaction Payer is the party (borrower/debtor) whose account is debited to fulfill a repayment obligation in a loan or credit facility. It identifies the source of repayment funds in the transaction flow.

RepaymentTransactionPayerAccountReferenc.description: A Repayment Transaction Payer Account is the specific debtor account from which repayment funds are debited in a loan or credit repayment transaction

RepaymentTransactionPayerBankReference.description: A Repayment Transaction Payer Bank Reference is the unique identifier assigned by the debtors (payers) servicing bank to a repayment transaction

RepaidAmount.description: A Repaid Amount is the actual sum of money that has already been paid by the borrower toward fulfilling repayment obligations under a loan or credit facility.

RepaymentTransactionValueDate.description: A Repayment Transaction Value Date is the date on which the repayment transaction is considered effective for interest calculation, settlement, and accounting purposes.

RepaymentOutstandingAmount.description: Repayment Outstanding Amount is the remaining unpaid balance that the borrower still owes under a loan or credit facility after accounting for all repayments already made

LastRepaymentDate.description: The terminal due date in the repayment schedule when the borrowers final installment (principal, interest, fees, or charges) is payable.It marks the end of the repayment lifecycle and is derived from the First Repayment Date, Repayment Frequency, and Repayment Period.

NextRepaymentDate.description: Next Repayment Date is the upcoming scheduled calendar date on which the borrower must make their next repayment obligation under the loan arrangement.It is a dynamic attribute that shifts forward after each repayment is executed, ensuring continuity of the repayment lifecycle.
